channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Madden NFL 27 Preview - Bear Down
item.content:encoded:
A new year means a new Madden NFL, and EA Sports has a lot to cover this year with the new version, as they tout their big, and probably expensive Persona Engine.

We got to see Madden NFL 27 up close and personal at a special Opening Drive event in Chicago, where we also learned about what fans can expect going into the launch later this year.

The foundation of Madden NFL 27 is being placed within the core idea of transforming your Franchise. Each NFL player will have their own distinctive personality that informs their decisions, and your league will, in turn, change with every decision you make.

These sorts of unique interactions produce unpredictable outcomes, and they vary depending on who in the league you deal with. There’s a new Free Agency Frenzy mode that adds a competitive edge to the league as well through a simultaneous bidding system.


Another new feature that branches off from the Superstar legacy is the new G.O.A.T. (Greatest of All Time) Career Journey. What this means is that performance, progression, and choice play a big part in the player’s journey as they go from high school to the eventual NFL Hall of Fame.

This includes a number of position-based skill drives that unlock new career choices as your status within Madden 27 rises. There’s also new positions including Tight End, Defensive Lineman, and Safety that add more dynamics to each season.

These dynamics extend to the gameplay in Madden NFL 27. In an effort to create authentic physicality, the game has added more realistic hand-fighting, jostling, and leverage when it comes to separation and coverage. 

Things like timing-based catching and refined contested interactions, along with improved short-yard mechanics and specific plays will make the difference between scoring or fumbling. 


New game plan adjustments have been added to fine-tune your team and the way they react. There are also streamlined pre-play controls that make adjustments incredibly intuitive. For example, smarter receiver logic can keep plays alive, while advanced coverage systems help defenses adapt in real time.

To put it simply, there’s complete control over executing team strategy during each snap.

But it wouldn’t be Madden without Ultimate Team. This time around, the system has been revamped, and upgrades are tied to a skill points system. 

What this means is that players earn skill points by completing player-specific objectives or by exchanging items. You can choose the player you want, increase their potential with new items called Evos, and ultimately create a roster that evolves as you get deeper into Madden 27.

As with EA Sports College Football 27, Madden 27 has been given its fair share of presentation upgrades. One of the more interesting upgrades comes in the form of a focus on small details – things like dangling mouthpieces, helmets that move while the player is in motion, and pad sizes have been adjusted to real life.

Dynamic weather is also a focus here, with rain and snow conditions leading to player degradation in real time as the game becomes harder to play in these extreme situations. New skyboxes and sun angles create a sense of realism as well, and it truly does look nice.

These advancements are capped off with Madden 27 hitting 60fps on Nintendo Switch 2, and a staggering 100+fps on PlayStation 5 Pro. It’s incredibly smooth and crisp on the visual front, all of that considered. 

The presentation wouldn’t be complete without the sounds of the NFL. Stadium traditions have been expanded to include things like Denver’s skydiving routine, as well as new introductions, drone shows, and holiday overlays to mix up the standard games from time to time.

Madden NFL 27 seems primed to deliver many of the things fans have been wanting for years.

As we approach the game’s August 13 release date across all platforms, it’s clear that Madden 27 has a lot going for it. Between the wealth of player choice, team management and skill paths, it’s more than just playing a game of football than ever before.

With that in mind, it’ll be interesting to see just how deep all these systems go and if their new engine can live up to all of those promises. 

TechRaptor was invited to preview Madden NFL 27 at an event by the publisher. All screenshots were provided by the publisher.
 

Erren is a staff writer for TechRaptor whose writing experience stems from years of helming Nova Crystallis – a website dedicated to everything Square Enix… More about Erren



https://techraptor.net/gaming/previews/madden-nfl-27-preview-bear-down
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: EA Sports College Football 27 Preview - School Days
item.content:encoded:
Things seem to be heating up for college football fans with the full reveal of EA Sports College Football 27 now in view. After a record-breaking release in previous years, we got to go hands-on with the game and detail all the new features happening this year.

While much of College Football 27 is spent playing the games themselves, there’s a whole system of micromanaging surrounding everything you do on the field. This is topped off by the new Dynasty Blueprint system, which allows the player to put everything together, such as playable NIL (name, image, and likeness), expanded couch systems, and the use of a currency known as Dynasty Points to upgrade sports facilities, recruitment, and staffing. 


It’s a game within a game, with the ultimate goal of achieving a fully fleshed-out Heisman Trophy Ceremony. 

Further updates to College Football this year include Road to Glory, which features new playable positions such as Tight End, Safety, and Edge. There’s also a new Legacy Score system that tracks all player decisions and will eventually impact your draft stock and reputation. 

The player builder system has also been retooled with a more flexible skill progression, physique-based caps, and points tied to player Max Potential. There’s even an academic element to it, where you’ll be tasked with taking on real tests and quizzes about sports and even real core school subjects. It almost feels like a RPG in that sense, as everything you do off-field has such a weighted importance now.


College Football 27 is also updating Road to the College Football Playoff by adding unlockable playbooks, crowd themes, and alternate uniforms that can be earned by completing team-specific tasks. 

Player freedom and progression seem to be a key part of College Football 27, with the New Upgrade System added to College Ultimate Team. Things have been redesigned to allow players to develop over time with the introduction of Skill Points – again, similar to a typical RPG – along with archetype-driven progression and attributes that can be upgraded accordingly. 

These quality-of-life improvements are a huge step forward in terms of streamlining progression, and establishing that player choice is more fluid on top of the more open, and sometimes chaotic gameplay, compared to something polished like Madden. 


One of the best modes is returning to College Football with the addition of the Mascot Mashup. In this mode, players can compete locally or online with full teams of mascots from each college team.

The fun part is that each mascot will be topped off with a 99 OVR (skill rating) and go up against other mascot squads. So you can imagine a full team of Hoosier the Bison going around, and have them face off against a bunch of Oregon Ducks. There are 100 mascots that can be unlocked in total, with ten available out of the gate. Those unlockables will come from winning a non-simulated Play Now game with each school’s mascot program.

Gameplay isn’t the only thing being upgraded for College Football 27. There have been a lot of visual and material graphics changes as well.

Dynamic weather will now be out in force and is based on real regional forecasts, which feed into the game intensity. Think commentary and how the game is actually played. Things will become tough when there’s a bunch of snow on the field.


Other additions include 20 new marching band song covers, expanded school traditions, trophies, drone and light shows, and even the student crowds are more excited for the game. The developers at EA Sports seem to be pulling out all the stops to make the atmosphere of College Football 27 feel as authentic as possible.

There’s a lot to unpack here, but seeing the game in action and observing the amount of depth that is going into the systems feels like just the tip of the iceberg. College Football 27 promises to be a wide-open, more creative experience, and it feels like all those little details and management outside of the field are going to matter.

Things like the team builder, customizing the crowd and stadium, along with coaches having their own value – these are just a handful of many that lend to the philosophy of being authentic to real college football. 

It’s safe to say things will be interesting to watch when the full game drops on July 9 for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and, for the first time ever – PC.

TechRaptor was invited to an event to preview College Football 27 by the publisher. All screenshots were provided by the publisher.

Erren is a staff writer for TechRaptor whose writing experience stems from years of helming Nova Crystallis – a website dedicated to everything Square Enix… More about Erren



https://techraptor.net/gaming/previews/college-football-27-preview-school-days
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
